R Notebook

library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex)
test_data = test_data |> dplyr::mutate(y_bin = test_data$f2, y_cens = ifelse(y > 5000, 5000, y), censoring = (y > 5000))

levels(test_data$f1) |> print()
[1] "Biscoe"    "Dream"     "Torgersen"
levels(test_data$f2) |> print()
[1] "female" "male"  
nrow(test_data) |> print()
[1] 333
tic = Sys.time()
bar = breg(y_cens ~ 1, 
           data = test_data, 
           family = "right_censored_linear",
           #family = "normal_linear",
           chains = 2,
           iter = 1000
           )
toc = Sys.time()
print(toc - tic)
Time difference of 0.5784819 secs
as.matrix(bar$stanfit)[1:4,]
          parameters
iterations delta_x1 delta_Intercept    sigma       x1
      [1,] 1.662953      0.05319733 344.3848 40.86082
      [2,] 1.495605     -0.16939556 365.5683 39.00933

stanmodels[["right_censored_linear_est"]]
S4 class stanmodel 'right_censored_linear_est' coded as follows:
data {
  int<lower=1> N;  // number of non-censored observations
  int<lower=1> Ncens;  // number of censored observations
  vector[N] Y;  // response variable (non-censored observations)
  real Ymax; // max observed value of Y (censoring point)
  real Ymean; // sample mean of response variable
  int<lower=1> K;  // number of population-level effects
  matrix[N, K] X;  // design matrix for non-censored data
  matrix[Ncens, K] Xcens; // design matrix for censored data
  vector[K] Xcol_scale; // factors for converting scaled effects
  int prior_only; // should the likelihood be ignored?
  real<lower=0> prior_scale; // scale of prior distribution on standarized coefficients (delta's)
}
parameters {
  vector[K] delta;  // standardized coefficients
  real delta0;  // standardized intercept
  real<lower=0> sigma;  // dispersion parameter
}
transformed parameters {
  vector[K] b;  // actual regression coefficients
  real b0;  // actual intercept
  b = sigma*delta./Xcol_scale;
  b0 = Ymean + sigma*delta0;
}
model {
  // likelihood including constants
  if (!prior_only) {
    target += normal_id_glm_lpdf(Y | X, b0, b, sigma); // non-censored data
    target += normal_lccdf(Ymax | b0 + Xcens*b, sigma); // censored data
  }
  // priors including constants
  target += cauchy_lpdf(delta0 | 0, prior_scale);
  target += cauchy_lpdf(delta | 0, prior_scale);
  target += -2*log(sigma);
} 
quz = breg(y ~ x1, data = test_data, family = "normal_linear")

Xg = Matrix::bdiag(Xg_list)
Xg
glmm_matrix = function(formula, data){
  formula_info = parse_formula(formula)
  gfactor = names(formula_info$random)[1]
  gef_formula = paste(foo$lhs, "~", paste(formula_info$random[[gfactor]], collapse = " + ")) |> formula()
  
  Xg_list = list()
  for(i in 1:length(levels(data[,gfactor]))){
    data_subset = data[data[,gfactor] == levels(data[,gfactor])[[i]],]
    Xg_list[[i]] = model.matrix(gef_formula, data = data_subset)
  }
  Xg = bdiag(Xg_list)
  return(Xg)
}
glmm_matrix(height ~ age + (age | Seed), data = Loblolly)
